what_it_does: Design draft that specifies how to connect the existing lifter registry into corpus capture and broaden deterministic, code-driven structural extraction per file type.
observations: The lifter registry mechanism is built but not yet consumed during capture; this design's main job is to wire that dead lane.

Current lifter selection keys on projection id, which does not line up with existing seed lifters or code projections; the design adds a file-type/extension selection axis while keeping produces optional.
Lifted structure would attach to each file record as a new structure field via the existing **extra passthrough, requiring no corpus.py schema change.
The design splits model-side open-ended description from lifter-side exact enumeration and warns never to ask the model to list what code can extract.
TypeScript/JS has no parser in the repo; the recommended first pass is regex extractors, with tree-sitter flagged as the robust upgrade.
Existing Markdown seed lifters (frontmatter, links, blocks) must be migrated to declare extensions=.md or they would silently stop being selected.
Inbound link descriptions are treated as a corpus-level read-time fold over stored link_context, not as a lifter responsibility.
standouts: The lifter registry exists but is currently a dead lane; the design's core task is connecting it.
Selection shifts from projection-id-first to file-type-first, decoupling most lifters from any projection.
structure rides free on records through existing **extra plumbing, so no corpus schema edit is needed.
TS/JS extraction is recommended as regex-first with a tree-sitter upgrade path, explicitly not punted.
concerns: Draft status with explicit assumptions and open questions; several decisions need confirmation.
Regex-based TS extraction is labeled inferred-complete, not verified-complete, so it may miss exotic syntax.
Adding extensions as a required selector without migrating the three seed lifters would make them silently extract nothing.
A declarative regex match selector applied to path or file head could be brittle for extension-less scripts.
Attaching all applicable lifter outputs to every file record could increase record size, though it is deterministic and resume-safe.
notes: A draft design for wiring the already-built lifter registry into corpus capture and expanding deterministic structural extraction by file type.
questions: Is the proposed decoupling of produces from most lifters aligned with the project's intent, or should every lifter remain tied to a produced_by:code projection?
Should the inbound-link description rollup live as a read over stored structure, or should it feed the relation_types/vector substrate so find_relations can range over it?
Given the TS correctness requirement, should the build start directly with tree-sitter instead of regex?
Will the model digest ever need to consume or reference the deterministic structure field, or must they remain strictly separate?
Do the existing seed lifters already have an implicit .md-only assumption that simply needs to be made explicit?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a tentative design draft for the deterministic-extraction layer and the wiring plan, and it behaves as such.
apparent_themes: Deterministic code extraction over model enumeration
Reuse existing mechanisms instead of parallel stores
Open-ended model description vs exact structural facts
File-drop extensibility for new file types
intention_signals: It is trying to make corpus records combine a model-generated digest with exact, code-extracted structural facts, while keeping the extraction set open to new file types through a file-discovered registry.
novelty: It treats lifters primarily as file-type structural extractors rather than projection producers, and it proposes a read-time corpus-level fold for inbound link descriptions instead of storing relations during capture.
contribution: Contributes a concrete wiring plan and schema extension for attaching deterministic structure to corpus records, a catalog of candidate per-file-type lifters, and explicit parser reuse/build decisions including a regex-now/tree-sitter-later path for TypeScript.
summary_for_embedding: Design draft for the lifters deterministic-extraction layer that wires the existing file-discovered lifter registry into corpus capture, adds file-type/extension selection, attaches extracted structure to records alongside the model digest, and catalogs Python/TypeScript/Markdown/JSON/YAML lifters with parser reuse/build decisions.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make code structure extracted deterministically available in the corpus without relying on the model, while keeping the registry open to new file types via file drops.
